column1: 2004-06-26‚Äé"Last Night was  The Best  concert i have ever been to
column2: including TRAVIS.   Jamie Cullum was absolutly amazing
column3: the 2 supports were also very good (Lucie Silvas and Tom Baxter). Jaime took every song form his Twenty Something album and made each of them completly original. Covers of ""Frontin"" and ""High and Dry"" were inspirational. Then
column4: taking the whole audience and getting us to sing a 3 part harmony (which sounded amazing)... pure magic. His 2 encores were top quality. He is undoubtably the greatest artist in the last 4 years to perform in the Pavailions! and i think thousands of other people would agree with me.  He Almost destroyed his piano constantly beating it with his hands
column7: feet and pretty much anything he could get his hands on. Yet it still stayed perfectly in tune. His ""good luck carpet"" kept the audience entertained - Though really it wasn't needed.  Acapello
column11: amazing drumming on the piano the concert had it all.   I am amazed at how little this account has come to
column12: but words can not describe the brilliance of it. There just aren't enough of concerts of this standard coming to Plymouth."
